description: Closes when all grading, drainage, irrigation, sod/planting, and exterior finish-grade elements are installed, tested, and accepted by client — lot is move-in ready from the ground plane out.

categories.name: Pre-Install Verification
items: Final grade elevations confirmed against civil/grading plan — positive drainage away from foundation minimum 6 inches in first 10 feet per IRC R401.3
All underground utilities (irrigation sleeves, low-voltage, gas, electrical) located and flagged before any digging begins
Landscape plan reviewed and approved by client — plant species, sizes (caliper/height), quantities, and placement confirmed in writing
Irrigation design plan on file — head layout, zone map, GPM per zone, and backflow preventer location confirmed
Sod or seed variety confirmed with client (e.g., Zoysia, Bermuda, St. Augustine) and availability verified with supplier
Any HOA or Baldwin County landscape buffers or tree-preservation requirements verified and flagged on plan
All hardscape (driveway, walkways, patios) fully complete and cured before bed preparation and sod install begins

categories.name: Grading & Drainage
items: Rough grade scraped, debris removed, and subgrade compacted to accept topsoil or sod bed
Topsoil depth minimum 4 inches across all turf areas, 6 inches in planting beds — verified by probe in at least 6 locations
Swales graded and confirmed to carry stormwater to street or detention area without pooling within 24 hours of a 1-inch rain event
French drains or area drains installed per plan — inlet grates set flush to grade, outfall confirmed daylighting clear of structure
Erosion control measures (silt fence, check dams) removed after final grade is stabilized
Final grade confirmed at all foundation vents, AC pad, and HVAC line sets — no soil contact with wood framing or stucco/fiber cement

categories.name: Irrigation Installation
items: Backflow preventer installed per Alabama Dept. of Environmental Management (ADEM) requirements and Baldwin County code — accessible for annual testing
All mainline and lateral lines installed at minimum 8-inch depth; sleeved under all hardscape at minimum 12-inch depth
Each zone mapped and labeled on controller — zone number, area served, and head type recorded on as-built zone map
Head type and spacing appropriate for zone: rotors on turf, drip emitters or bubblers in planting beds — no overspray onto structure, fencing, or hardscape
Static water pressure at meter and dynamic pressure at zone heads measured and recorded — head pressure within manufacturer's specified operating range (typically 30–45 PSI)
All zones run full cycle and inspected by superintendent: full coverage confirmed, no misting from over-pressure, no dry spots greater than 12 inches in diameter
Rain/freeze sensor installed and wired to controller — tested for automatic shutoff function

categories.name: Planting & Sod Installation
items: All planting bed edges cleanly defined — beds mulched with minimum 3-inch depth hardwood or pine straw (client-selected), pulled back 2 inches from plant stems
Trees planted with root flare at or slightly above finished grade — no trunk burial; staked only if caliper is under 2 inches or site is high-wind exposure
Shrubs and groundcover installed per plan spacing; root balls confirmed intact and watered in at time of install
Sod installed within 24 hours of delivery — no yellowed or dried rolls accepted; joints staggered, edges cut tight to beds and hardscape
Sod rolled after install to ensure full soil contact — no air pockets or lifted edges visible
All plant material is Florida #1 grade or better (FNGLA standard) — superintendent verified species and size against landscape plan at delivery

categories.name: Exterior Site Furnishings & Lighting
items: Mailbox installed per USPS and subdivision/HOA requirements — post plumb and set in concrete
Low-voltage landscape lighting installed per plan — transformer load calculated and within 80% of rated capacity; all fixtures aimed and operational
House numbers installed and visible from street per Baldwin County addressing requirements
Any address monument, entry columns, or decorative fencing confirmed complete, plumb, and matching approved design
Outdoor shower, hose bibs, and exterior GFCI outlets confirmed functional and not obstructed by planting

categories.name: Quality & Punch
items: Full perimeter walk with superintendent: no exposed bare soil in turf areas, no standing water, no hardscape damage from landscape equipment
All tree wrap, burlap, wire, and nursery tags removed from installed plant material
Concrete driveway, walkways, and pool coping cleaned of all soil, mulch, and plant debris — pressure washed if needed
Irrigation controller programmed with initial seasonal schedule, labeled, and manual left with client or stored in control box
Landscape contractor punch list issued and all items closed before client walkthrough

categories.name: Client Walkthrough & Closeout
items: Client walkthrough completed — superintendent and landscape contractor present; client signs landscape acceptance form
Irrigation orientation completed with client: zone map reviewed, manual operation demonstrated, rain sensor tested, winterization procedure explained
Plant warranty documentation provided to client — minimum 1-year warranty on trees and shrubs standard for Palmetto builds; warranty terms in writing
Landscape contractor's final lien waiver received and filed
As-built irrigation zone map scanned and uploaded to project Drive folder
Turf and plant care instructions (watering schedule, fertilization, mowing height by species) provided to client in writing
